name: Users can create Fabric items
description: Users can use production-ready features to create Fabric items
notes: Uncontrolled creation leads to sprawl; restrict to licensed/approved groups
ms_docs_anchor: Users can create Fabric items

name: Users can create Ontology items (preview)
description: Users can create ontologies for enterprise semantics
recommended_setting: On only for early adopters / pilot group
notes: Preview feature; low risk if left broad but no urgency
ms_docs_anchor: Users can create Ontology (preview) items

name: User can create Graph (preview)
description: Visualise data with a Graph for richer context
notes: Preview; limited blast radius
ms_docs_anchor: User can create Graph (preview)

name: Users can create Digital Twin Builder items (preview)
description: Users can create digital twin builder items
notes: Preview only; not needed for most orgs
ms_docs_anchor: Users can create Digital Twin Builder (preview) items

name: Users can discover and create org apps (preview)
description: Let users create org apps as items
notes: Preview — enable when stable
ms_docs_anchor: Users can discover and create org apps (preview)

name: Product Feedback
description: Microsoft can prompt users for in-product survey feedback
notes: No data risk; improves product quality
ms_docs_anchor: Product Feedback

name: Users informed of upcoming conferences
description: Inform users of conferences featuring Microsoft Fabric
notes: Cosmetic — no security risk; disable to reduce distraction
ms_docs_anchor: Users can be informed of upcoming conferences featuring Microsoft Fabric when they are logged in to Fabric

name: ML models serve real-time predictions (preview)
description: Users can create real-time predictions from model endpoints
recommended_setting: Off until ML ops readiness confirmed
notes: External-facing ML endpoints need governance before enabling
ms_docs_anchor: ML models can serve real-time predictions from API endpoints (preview)

name: Detect anomalies in Real-Time Intelligence (Preview)
description: Use statistical algorithms to detect real-time anomalies
notes: Feature flag; enable when using Eventstream/KQL
ms_docs_anchor: Detect anomalies in Real-Time Intelligence (Preview)

name: Users can create dbt job items (preview)
description: Import, author and execute dbt projects in Fabric
notes: Preview; safe to enable for engineering
ms_docs_anchor: Users can create dbt job items (preview)

name: Enable Operations Agents (Preview)
description: Create operations agents using Azure OpenAI
recommended_setting: Off — review data boundary requirements first
notes: Processes data via Azure AI Bot Service; potential EU Data Boundary implications
ms_docs_anchor: Enable Operations Agents (Preview)

name: All Power BI users can see 'Set alert' button
description: All Power BI users see Set alert button in reports
notes: UI visibility only; actual creation needs Fabric license
ms_docs_anchor: All Power BI users can see "Set alert" button to create Fabric Activator alerts

name: Users can create Plan items (preview)
description: Create integrated planning items in Fabric
notes: Preview — evaluate when stable
ms_docs_anchor: Users can create Plan (preview) items

name: Publish 'Get Help' information
description: Users can go to internal help/support from Power BI menu
notes: Without internal help links users go to public forums; increases support ticket volume
ms_docs_anchor: Publish "Get Help" information

name: Receive email/Teams notifications for service outages
description: Mail-enabled groups receive outage/incident notifications
recommended_setting: On — assign to BI ops/admin group
notes: Missing incident notifications causes blind-spot during outages
ms_docs_anchor: Receive email and Teams notifications for service outages or incidents

name: Users can try Microsoft Fabric paid features
description: Users can try Fabric paid features free for 60 days
recommended_setting: Off or scoped to approved pilots
notes: Uncontrolled trials generate capacity costs and data sprawl
ms_docs_anchor: Users can try Microsoft Fabric paid features

name: Show custom message before publishing reports
description: Users see a custom message before publishing a report
notes: Reduces accidental sharing of sensitive reports; cheap governance win
ms_docs_anchor: Show a custom message before publishing reports

name: Allow tenant/domain admins to override workspace assignments
description: Admins can reassign workspaces between domains
notes: Useful for large tenants with domain governance
ms_docs_anchor: Allow tenant and domain admins to override workspace assignments (preview)

name: Create workspaces
description: Users can create app workspaces to collaborate
notes: Unrestricted workspace creation is the top cause of Power BI sprawl
ms_docs_anchor: Create workspaces

name: Use semantic models across workspaces
description: Users can use semantic models across workspaces via Build permission
recommended_setting: On — enables reuse and single source of truth
notes: Good practice; turning off breaks shared model architectures
ms_docs_anchor: Use semantic models across workspaces

name: Block users from reassigning personal workspaces
description: Prevent users reassigning My Workspace from Premium to shared
recommended_setting: On if Premium capacity is licensed
notes: Prevents personal workspaces from silently moving off Premium SKU
ms_docs_anchor: Block users from reassigning personal workspaces (My Workspace)

name: Define workspace retention period
description: Define retention period before deleted workspaces are permanently removed
recommended_setting: On — set 90 days for business-critical workspaces
notes: 7 days is too short to recover from accidental deletion
ms_docs_anchor: Define workspace retention period

name: Auto-convert reports to PBIR format (preview)
description: Automatically convert reports to PBIR format after editing
recommended_setting: On for teams using Git integration
notes: Enables source control-friendly format; no risk if using PBIR workflow
ms_docs_anchor: Automatically convert and store reports using Power BI enhanced metadata format (PBIR) (preview)

name: Fabric item recovery
description: Deleted items are retained for a defined period
notes: Without this, deleting items is permanent; critical for DR
ms_docs_anchor: Fabric item recovery

name: Allow users to apply sensitivity labels
description: Sensitivity labels from Purview can be applied to content
notes: Core GDPR/compliance control; governs data classification across exports
ms_docs_anchor: Allow users to apply sensitivity labels for content

name: Apply sensitivity labels from data sources
description: Sensitivity labels from supported data sources are inherited
recommended_setting: On — inherits labels from certified sources
notes: Reduces manual labelling burden; propagates governance automatically
ms_docs_anchor: Apply sensitivity labels from data sources to their data in Power BI

name: Auto-apply sensitivity labels to downstream content
description: Labels are applied to downstream content when source changes
notes: Without this, downstream reports lose classification when source changes
ms_docs_anchor: Automatically apply sensitivity labels to downstream content

name: Allow workspace admins to override auto-applied labels
description: Workspace admins can change/remove auto-applied sensitivity labels
notes: Allowing override weakens automated governance chain
ms_docs_anchor: Allow workspace admins to override automatically applied sensitivity labels

name: Restrict protected labels from org-wide link sharing
description: Prevent content with protection settings being shared org-wide via link
recommended_setting: On — prevents org-wide link sharing of sensitive content
notes: Gaps in this allow confidential data to reach all internal users unintentionally
ms_docs_anchor: Restrict content with protected labels from being shared via link with everyone in your organization

name: Domain admins can set default sensitivity labels (preview)
description: Domain admins can set default sensitivity labels for their domains
notes: Useful governance tool; low risk to enable
ms_docs_anchor: Domain admins can set default sensitivity labels for their domains (preview)

name: Allow Microsoft Purview to secure AI interactions
description: Purview can access/process prompts and responses for compliance
notes: Required for AI prompt/response auditing; critical for compliance orgs
ms_docs_anchor: Allow Microsoft Purview to secure AI interactions

name: External data sharing
description: Users can share read-only links to OneLake data externally
recommended_setting: Off or restricted to approved teams
notes: Sharing OneLake data externally with no controls risks data leakage
ms_docs_anchor: External data sharing

name: Users can accept external data shares
description: Users can accept read-only links to data from other tenants
notes: Unrestricted inbound external shares = unvetted external data in tenant
ms_docs_anchor: Users can accept external data shares

name: Guest users can access Microsoft Fabric
description: Guest users in Entra directory can access Fabric
recommended_setting: On if B2B collaboration is needed; restrict via Entra
notes: Needed for B2B; but align with Entra external collaboration policy
ms_docs_anchor: Guest users can access Microsoft Fabric

name: Users can invite guest users to collaborate
description: Users can collaborate with external people by sharing Fabric items
notes: Any user inviting external guests creates shadow IT and Entra noise
ms_docs_anchor: Users can invite guest users to collaborate through item sharing and permissions

name: Guest users can browse and access Fabric content
description: Users can invite guests to browse and request access to content
recommended_setting: On only after guest governance policy defined
notes: Enables browsing without explicit invite; moderate risk
ms_docs_anchor: Guest users can browse and access Fabric content

name: Users can see guest users in suggested people lists
description: Users see both org and guest users in suggested-people lists
notes: Prevents accidental sharing to guest by autocomplete
ms_docs_anchor: Users can see guest users in lists of suggested people

name: Publish to web
description: People can publish public reports accessible without authentication
recommended_setting: Off — keep disabled unless specific public-dashboard use case
notes: Publicly accessible reports with no auth; high risk if enabled broadly
ms_docs_anchor: Publish to web

name: Copy and paste visuals
description: Users can copy visuals and paste as static images externally
notes: Standard usability feature; no significant risk
ms_docs_anchor: Copy and paste visuals

name: Export to Excel
description: Users can export data from visualisations to an Excel file
recommended_setting: On — can restrict to specific groups if sensitive data
notes: Excel export can extract full underlying data; consider RLS adequacy
ms_docs_anchor: Export to Excel

name: Export to .csv
description: Users can export data from a tile, visual or paginated report to .csv
notes: CSV strips all access controls; underlying data fully exposed
ms_docs_anchor: Export to .csv

name: Download reports
description: Users can download .pbix files and paginated reports
recommended_setting: Restrict to report owners / specific group
notes: Downloaded .pbix files contain embedded data and M/DAX logic
ms_docs_anchor: Download reports

name: Work with semantic models in Excel via live connection
description: Users can use Analyze in Excel and XMLA live connections
recommended_setting: On — enables Analyze in Excel; valuable for self-service
notes: Live connection; data stays server-side
ms_docs_anchor: Users can work with semantic models in Excel using a live connection

name: Export reports as PowerPoint or PDF
description: Users can export reports as PowerPoint files or PDF documents
notes: Snapshot exports; no model exposure
ms_docs_anchor: Export reports as PowerPoint presentations or PDF documents

name: Export reports as MHTML documents
description: Users can export paginated reports as MHTML documents
notes: Paginated only; low risk
ms_docs_anchor: Export reports as MHTML documents

name: Export reports as Word documents
description: Users can export paginated reports as Word documents
notes: Paginated only; low risk
ms_docs_anchor: Export reports as Word documents

name: Export reports as XML documents
description: Users can export paginated reports as XML documents
notes: Paginated only; low risk
ms_docs_anchor: Export reports as XML documents

name: Export reports as image files
description: Users can use the API to export reports as image files
notes: Image export; no underlying data
ms_docs_anchor: Export reports as image files

name: Print dashboards and reports
description: Users can print dashboards and reports
notes: Standard usability
ms_docs_anchor: Print dashboards and reports

name: Certification
description: Specific groups can certify items as trusted sources
notes: Without certification, users can't distinguish trusted from untrusted content
ms_docs_anchor: Certification

name: Endorse master data
description: Specific groups can endorse items as core data sources
notes: Governs master data discoverability; reduces duplication
ms_docs_anchor: Endorse master data

name: Users can set up email subscriptions
description: Users can create email subscriptions to reports and dashboards
notes: Subscriptions deliver snapshots; low risk
ms_docs_anchor: Users can set up email subscriptions

name: B2B guest users can set up email subscriptions
description: B2B guest users can set up and be subscribed to email subscriptions
recommended_setting: Off unless B2B is active use case
notes: Limited exposure; review alongside B2B policy
ms_docs_anchor: B2B guest users can set up and be subscribed to email subscriptions

name: Users can send email subscriptions to external users
description: Users can subscribe external users to email subscriptions
recommended_setting: Off — prevent data leaving tenant via email
notes: Sending report snapshots externally with no DLP controls
ms_docs_anchor: Users can send email subscriptions to external users

name: Featured content
description: Users can promote their published content to Power BI Home Featured section
notes: Promotes visibility of quality content
ms_docs_anchor: Featured content

name: Allow connections to featured tables
description: Users can access and calculate data from featured tables in Excel
notes: Read-only connection; low risk
ms_docs_anchor: Allow connections to featured tables

name: Allow shareable links to everyone in organisation
description: This setting grants access to anyone in the organisation with the link
notes: Internal only; acceptable for most orgs
ms_docs_anchor: Allow shareable links to grant access to everyone in your organization

name: Enable Microsoft Teams integration
description: People can access features associated with Teams and Power BI integration
notes: Valuable integration; no significant risk
ms_docs_anchor: Enable Microsoft Teams integration

name: Install Power BI app for Teams automatically
description: Power BI app for Teams installed automatically for users
notes: Improves adoption; no security risk
ms_docs_anchor: Install Power BI app for Microsoft Teams automatically

name: Enable Power BI add-in for PowerPoint
description: People can embed Power BI data into PowerPoint presentations
notes: Live embed; no data copy
ms_docs_anchor: Enable Power BI add-in for PowerPoint

name: Allow DirectQuery connections to Power BI semantic models
description: DirectQuery connections allow users to build on existing semantic models
notes: Controlled by Build permission on source model
ms_docs_anchor: Allow DirectQuery connections to Power BI semantic models

name: Guest users work with shared semantic models in their tenants
description: Authorized guest users can work with shared datasets in their own tenants
notes: External parties querying internal models; high data-exposure risk
ms_docs_anchor: Guest users can work with shared semantic models in their own tenants

name: Allow specific users to turn on external data sharing
description: Controls whether users can turn on external data sharing option
recommended_setting: Off or restrict to data owners group
notes: Effectively controls whether cross-tenant data sharing is possible
ms_docs_anchor: Allow specific users to turn on external data sharing

name: Users with read/write permission can download notebook data
description: Users with read/write permission can download data from notebook outputs
notes: Notebook data download bypasses report-level export controls
ms_docs_anchor: Users with read or write permission can download data from notebooks

name: Make promoted content discoverable
description: Users who promote content can make it discoverable without access
notes: Improves self-service findability
ms_docs_anchor: Make promoted content discoverable

name: Make certified content discoverable
description: Users who certify content can make it discoverable without access
notes: Drives adoption of governed content
ms_docs_anchor: Make certified content discoverable

name: Discover content
description: Allow users to find and request access to discoverable content
notes: Enables access requests; positive governance behaviour
ms_docs_anchor: Discover content

name: Create template organisational apps
description: Users can create template apps that use semantic models
recommended_setting: On for Centre of Excellence team
notes: Useful for deploying governed app templates
ms_docs_anchor: Create template organizational apps

name: Push apps to end users
description: Users can share apps directly with end users without AppSource install
recommended_setting: On for IT/BI team distributing governed apps
notes: Reduces friction for standard app rollout
ms_docs_anchor: Push apps to end users

name: Publish apps to entire organisation
description: Users can publish apps to the entire organisation
notes: Any workspace admin can publish to all users; risk of ungoverned apps
ms_docs_anchor: Publish apps to the entire organization

name: Allow XMLA endpoints and Analyze in Excel with on-prem models
description: Users can use Excel with on-premises Power BI semantic models
recommended_setting: On — required for XMLA tooling (Tabular Editor, DAX Studio)
notes: XMLA read is essential for BI development toolchain
ms_docs_anchor: Allow XMLA endpoints and Analyze in Excel with on-premises semantic models

name: Semantic Model Execute Queries REST API
description: Users can query semantic models via DAX through REST APIs
notes: Enables programmatic DAX queries; low risk with proper SP governance
ms_docs_anchor: Semantic Model Execute Queries REST API

name: Users can use Power BI MCP server endpoint (preview)
description: Users can connect MCP clients to Power BI
notes: MCP exposes model metadata to AI clients; needs SP governance
ms_docs_anchor: Users can use the Power BI Model Context Protocol server endpoint (preview)

name: Use ArcGIS Maps for Power BI
description: Users can use Esri ArcGIS Maps visualisation
recommended_setting: On if Esri is used; Off otherwise
notes: Third-party data; low risk if not using Esri
ms_docs_anchor: Use ArcGIS Maps for Power BI

name: Use global search for Power BI
description: Users can use the global search bar at the top of the page
notes: Standard usability
ms_docs_anchor: Use global search for Power BI

name: Users can use Azure Maps visual
description: Users can create and view the Azure Maps visual
notes: Azure Maps; data processing by Microsoft
ms_docs_anchor: Users can use the Azure Maps visual

name: Azure Maps data processed outside tenant geography
description: Data sent to Azure Maps can be processed outside tenant region
recommended_setting: Off for EU Data Boundary tenants
notes: EU compliance — data processed outside boundary violates residency
ms_docs_anchor: Data sent to Azure Maps can be processed outside your tenant's geographic region, compliance boundary, or national cloud instance

name: Map and filled map visuals
description: Allow people to use the map and filled map visualisations
notes: Standard built-in visual
ms_docs_anchor: Map and filled map visuals

name: Integration with SharePoint and Microsoft Lists
description: Users can launch Power BI from SharePoint and Microsoft Lists
notes: Standard M365 integration
ms_docs_anchor: Integration with SharePoint and Microsoft Lists

name: Snowflake SSO
description: Enable SSO capability for Snowflake
recommended_setting: On if Snowflake is in data stack
notes: SSO improves security over shared credentials
ms_docs_anchor: Snowflake SSO

name: Google BigQuery SSO
description: Enable SSO capability for Google BigQuery
recommended_setting: On if BigQuery is in data stack
notes: SSO improves security over shared credentials
ms_docs_anchor: Google BigQuery SSO

name: Microsoft Entra SSO for data gateway
description: Users can use Entra SSO to authenticate to on-premises data gateways
notes: Stored gateway credentials are a security anti-pattern; Entra SSO is best practice
ms_docs_anchor: Microsoft Entra single sign-on for data gateway

name: Users can view Power BI files in OneDrive/SharePoint (preview)
description: Users can view Power BI files saved in OneDrive/SharePoint
notes: Improves collaboration; no extra data exposure
ms_docs_anchor: Users can view Power BI files saved in OneDrive and SharePoint (preview)

name: Enable granular access control for all data connections
description: Enforce strict access control for all data connection types
notes: Without this, shared items may use another user's data connection credentials
ms_docs_anchor: Enable granular access control for all data connections

name: Semantic models can export data to OneLake
description: Semantic models configured for OneLake integration can export import tables
notes: Enables OneLake integration; review data classification before enabling
ms_docs_anchor: Semantic models can export data to OneLake

name: Semantic model owners can auto-update from OneDrive/SharePoint
description: Semantic models can auto-update from OneDrive/SharePoint .pbix files
notes: Source controlled via OneDrive; low risk
ms_docs_anchor: Semantic model owners can choose to automatically update semantic models from files imported from OneDrive or SharePoint

name: Allow visuals created using Power BI SDK
description: Users can add, view, share and interact with custom visuals
notes: Standard; certified visuals setting governs trust
ms_docs_anchor: Allow visuals created using the Power BI SDK

name: Add and use certified visuals only (block uncertified)
description: Users can only add and use certified visuals
notes: Uncertified visuals can exfiltrate data to external endpoints
ms_docs_anchor: Add and use certified visuals only (block uncertified)

name: Allow downloads from custom visuals
description: Custom visuals can download information available to the visual
notes: Custom visual downloads = uncontrolled data export path
ms_docs_anchor: Allow downloads from custom visuals

name: AppSource Custom Visuals SSO
description: Enable SSO for AppSource custom visuals; Entra tokens sent to visual
recommended_setting: Off unless vetted AppSource visuals need Entra token
notes: Access tokens sent to third-party visual publishers
ms_docs_anchor: AppSource Custom Visuals SSO

name: Allow access to browser's local storage by custom visuals
description: Custom visuals can store information in the user's browser local storage
notes: Local storage by visuals is an uncommon need; disable by default
ms_docs_anchor: Allow access to the browser's local storage

name: Interact with and share R and Python visuals
description: Users can interact with and share visuals created with R or Python scripts
recommended_setting: On for data science teams; Off for non-technical orgs
notes: R/Python visuals run server-side; no client risk
ms_docs_anchor: Interact with and share R and Python visuals

name: Usage metrics for content creators
description: Users can see usage metrics for content they have permission to
notes: Essential for governance and adoption tracking
ms_docs_anchor: Usage metrics for content creators

name: Per-user data in usage metrics
description: Usage metrics expose display names and email addresses of users
recommended_setting: On — required for user-level adoption analysis
notes: PII in metrics; acceptable for internal BI governance
ms_docs_anchor: Per-user data in usage metrics for content creators

name: Show user data in Fabric Capacity Metrics app
description: Active user data including names/emails shown in Capacity Metrics app
notes: Needed for capacity management
ms_docs_anchor: Show user data in the Fabric Capacity Metrics app and reports

name: Azure Log Analytics connections for workspace admins
description: Workspace admins can configure Azure Log Analytics connections
recommended_setting: On for mature BI ops teams
notes: Enables detailed audit logging per workspace; requires Log Analytics workspace
ms_docs_anchor: Azure Log Analytics connections for workspace administrators

name: Workspace admins can turn on workspace monitoring (preview)
description: Workspace admins can turn on monitoring for their workspaces
notes: Preview; useful for activity auditing
ms_docs_anchor: Workspace admins can turn on monitoring for their workspaces (preview)

name: Microsoft can store query text to aid support
description: Query text stored securely for use during support investigations
notes: Disabling harms Microsoft's ability to support; recommend leaving on
ms_docs_anchor: Microsoft can store query text to aid in support investigations

name: Web content on dashboard tiles
description: Users can add and view web content tiles on Power BI dashboards
notes: Malicious web content can be embedded in dashboards visible to all users
ms_docs_anchor: Web content on dashboard tiles

name: Embed content in apps
description: Users can embed Power BI dashboards and reports in web applications
recommended_setting: On if ISV/embedded analytics in use
notes: Embed tokens required for ISV scenarios
ms_docs_anchor: Embed content in apps

name: Service principals can create workspaces, connections, pipelines
description: Service principals can create workspaces, connections, and deployment pipelines
recommended_setting: On — needed for CI/CD and automation
notes: Required for DevOps/ALM pipelines; govern via security group
ms_docs_anchor: Service principals can create workspaces, connections, and deployment pipelines

name: Service principals can call Fabric public APIs
description: Service principals can call Fabric public APIs with appropriate roles
notes: Needed for programmatic Fabric management; govern via SG
ms_docs_anchor: Service principals can call Fabric public APIs

name: Allow service principals to create and use profiles
description: Service principals can create and use profiles
notes: ISV-specific; enable only if multi-tenant embedding is in scope
ms_docs_anchor: Allow service principals to create and use profiles

name: Block ResourceKey Authentication
description: Block resource key based authentication for streaming semantic models
recommended_setting: On — disables insecure streaming resource keys
notes: Resource key auth on streaming datasets is a weaker auth method
ms_docs_anchor: Block ResourceKey Authentication

name: Service principals can access read-only admin APIs
description: Web apps can use service principals to authenticate to read-only admin APIs
recommended_setting: On for governance/CoE tooling (restrict to SG)
notes: Required for automated tenant scanning and governance tools
ms_docs_anchor: Service principals can access read-only admin APIs

name: Service principals can access admin APIs for updates
description: Web apps can use service principals to authenticate to admin APIs for updates
recommended_setting: Off unless automation requires write admin access
notes: Write access to all admin APIs via SP is very high privilege
ms_docs_anchor: Service principals can access admin APIs used for updates

name: Enhance admin APIs with detailed metadata
description: Admin API responses include detailed metadata (table/column names)
notes: Needed for tenant scanning (table/column names in scan results)
ms_docs_anchor: Enhance admin APIs responses with detailed metadata

name: Enhance admin APIs with DAX and mashup expressions
description: Admin API responses include DAX and M query expressions
recommended_setting: Off unless lineage/documentation automation is in scope
notes: Exposes DAX/M expressions — IP and logic in reports/models
ms_docs_anchor: Enhance admin APIs responses with DAX and mashup expressions

name: Create and use Gen1 dataflows
description: Users can create and use Gen1 dataflows
recommended_setting: On — legacy workloads may depend on this
notes: Gen2 is preferred but Gen1 still needed for compatibility
ms_docs_anchor: Create and use Gen1 dataflows

name: Publish template apps
description: Users can publish template apps for external distribution
notes: Publishing to AppSource; ISV-specific
ms_docs_anchor: Publish template apps

name: Install template apps
description: Users can install template apps created outside the organisation
notes: Standard for consuming certified template apps
ms_docs_anchor: Install template apps

name: Install template apps not listed in AppSource
description: Users with permission can install template apps not in AppSource
recommended_setting: Off — only allow vetted AppSource apps
notes: Unlisted template apps bypass AppSource security review
ms_docs_anchor: Install template apps not listed in AppSource

name: Review Q&A questions
description: Semantic model owners can review questions asked about their data
notes: Helps improve NLP model accuracy
ms_docs_anchor: Review questions

name: Synonym sharing
description: People can share Q&A synonyms with the organisation
notes: Useful for Q&A model improvement
ms_docs_anchor: Synonym sharing

name: Users with view permission can launch Explore
description: Users with view permission can do ad hoc analysis via Explore
notes: View-only ad hoc analysis; no model change risk
ms_docs_anchor: Users with view permission can launch Explore

name: Block republish and disable package refresh
description: Disable package refresh; only model owner can publish updates
notes: Without this, any workspace member can overwrite a production model
ms_docs_anchor: Block republish and disable package refresh

name: Tenant-level Private Link
description: Increase security by using a Private Link to access Fabric tenant
notes: Without private link, Fabric is accessible over public internet
ms_docs_anchor: Tenant-level Private Link

name: Block Public Internet Access
description: Block access to Fabric tenant via the public internet
recommended_setting: On only after Private Link configured
notes: Locks out all public access; must configure Private Link first
ms_docs_anchor: Block Public Internet Access

name: Configure workspace-level inbound network rules
description: Workspace admins can configure inbound private link access protection
notes: Granular network control per workspace; useful in regulated tenants
ms_docs_anchor: Configure workspace-level inbound network rules

name: Configure workspace-level outbound network rules
description: Workspace admins can configure outbound access protection
recommended_setting: On for DLP on data egress
notes: Controls outbound traffic from workspaces
ms_docs_anchor: Configure workspace-level outbound network rules

name: Configure workspace-level IP firewall rules (preview)
description: Workspace admins can configure IP firewall rules and trusted resource instances
notes: Granular IP control; useful in regulated tenants
ms_docs_anchor: Configure workspace-level IP firewall rules and trusted resource instances (preview)

name: Apply customer-managed keys
description: Users can configure workspace level encryption using customer-managed keys
recommended_setting: On for regulated industries requiring BYOK
notes: Default Microsoft-managed keys are acceptable; CMK required for some compliance frameworks
ms_docs_anchor: Apply customer-managed keys

name: Create and use Scorecards
description: Users can create and use Scorecards
notes: Standard KPI tracking feature
ms_docs_anchor: Create and use Scorecards

name: Help Power BI optimise your experience (UX experiments)
description: Users get minor UX variations the Power BI team is experimenting with
recommended_setting: Off — avoid unpredictable UI changes in production
notes: Preview UX experiments can confuse end users; disable for stability
ms_docs_anchor: Help Power BI optimize your experience

name: Share Fabric data with Microsoft 365 services
description: Fabric data can be stored and displayed in Microsoft 365 services
recommended_setting: On if same geography; review for cross-geo tenants
notes: Fabric metadata used in M365 search/Copilot recommendations; review for privacy policy alignment
ms_docs_anchor: Share Fabric data with your Microsoft 365 services

name: Receive notifications for top insights (preview)
description: Users can enable notifications for top insights in report settings
notes: Passive notification; no risk
ms_docs_anchor: Receive notifications for top insights (preview)

name: Show entry points for insights (preview)
description: Users can use entry points for requesting insights inside reports
notes: UI feature; low risk
ms_docs_anchor: Show entry points for insights (preview)

name: Create Datamarts (preview)
description: Users can create Datamarts
notes: Standard Fabric feature
ms_docs_anchor: Create Datamarts (preview)

name: Users can edit semantic models in Power BI service
description: Users can edit semantic models in the service (non-DirectLake)
recommended_setting: On for advanced self-service; Off for governed central models
notes: In-service editing bypasses Desktop/Git workflow; assess governance maturity
ms_docs_anchor: Users can edit semantic models in the Power BI service

name: Scale out queries for large semantic models
description: Queries distributed across replicas when volume is high
notes: Performance feature; no security risk
ms_docs_anchor: Scale out queries for large semantic models

name: Users can access OneLake data with external apps
description: Users can access OneLake data with apps external to Fabric
recommended_setting: On — needed for Databricks, custom apps, File Explorer
notes: External app access to OneLake; enforce item-level permissions
ms_docs_anchor: Users can access data stored in OneLake with apps external to Fabric

name: Use short-lived user-delegated SAS tokens
description: OneLake SAS tokens enable apps to access data via Entra identity
recommended_setting: On — more secure than long-lived tokens
notes: SAS tokens expire in <1hr; low risk; enables secure integrations
ms_docs_anchor: Use short-lived user-delegated SAS tokens

name: Authenticate with OneLake user-delegated SAS tokens
description: Allow applications to authenticate using a OneLake SAS token
recommended_setting: On if SAS token auth is needed by apps
notes: Tied to above; enable together
ms_docs_anchor: Authenticate with OneLake user-delegated SAS tokens

name: Users can sync data with OneLake File Explorer app
description: Users can use OneLake File Explorer to sync OneLake items to Windows
notes: Desktop sync tool; data stays within tenant permissions
ms_docs_anchor: Users can sync data in OneLake with the OneLake File Explorer app

name: Include end-user identifiers in OneLake diagnostic logs
description: OneLake diagnostic logs capture end user identifiable information
recommended_setting: On for audit/compliance; Off for strict privacy
notes: PII in logs; assess against data retention policy
ms_docs_anchor: Include end-user identifiers in OneLake diagnostic logs

name: Users can synchronise workspace items with Git
description: Users can import/export workspace items to Git repositories
notes: Enables ALM/source control; best practice for governed development
ms_docs_anchor: Users can synchronize workspace items with their Git repositories

name: Users can export items to Git repos in other geographies
description: Workspace and Git repository may reside in different geographies
recommended_setting: Off for EU Data Boundary tenants
notes: Cross-geo data export may violate data residency requirements
ms_docs_anchor: Users can export items to Git repositories in other geographical locations

name: Users can export items with sensitivity labels to Git
description: Users can export items with applied sensitivity labels to Git repos
recommended_setting: Off — sensitivity labels should not leave tenant boundary
notes: Sensitivity-labelled content exported to external Git repos loses protection
ms_docs_anchor: Users can export workspace items with applied sensitivity labels to Git repositories

name: Users can sync workspace items with GitHub
description: Users can select GitHub as their Git provider
notes: GitHub as Git provider; needs cross-geo export setting review
ms_docs_anchor: Users can sync workspace items with GitHub repositories

name: Users can use Copilot and Azure OpenAI features
description: Users can access Fabric features powered by Azure OpenAI including Copilot
recommended_setting: On for licensed users; review data boundary
notes: AI features send data to Azure OpenAI; EU Data Boundary compliance critical
ms_docs_anchor: Users can use Copilot and other features powered by Azure OpenAI

name: Users can access standalone cross-item Power BI Copilot (preview)
description: Users access a Copilot experience to find and analyse Fabric items
recommended_setting: On if Copilot is licensed and enabled
notes: Dependent on parent Copilot setting
ms_docs_anchor: Users can access a standalone, cross-item Power BI Copilot experience (preview)

name: Azure OpenAI data processed outside capacity geography
description: Data sent to Copilot can be processed outside capacity region
notes: EU Data Boundary violation risk; keep Off unless capacity is US/non-EU
ms_docs_anchor: Data sent to Azure OpenAI can be processed outside your capacity's geographic region, compliance boundary, or national cloud instance

name: Capacities can be designated as Fabric Copilot capacities
description: Capacity admins can designate capacities as Fabric Copilot capacities
notes: Billing/admin feature; no security risk
ms_docs_anchor: Capacities can be designated as Fabric Copilot capacities

name: Azure OpenAI data stored outside capacity geography
description: Data sent to Azure OpenAI can be stored outside capacity region
notes: Storage of prompts/responses outside EU boundary — compliance risk
ms_docs_anchor: Data sent to Azure OpenAI can be stored outside your capacity's geographic region, compliance boundary, or national cloud instance

name: Only show approved items in standalone Copilot (preview)
description: Only items marked as 'approved for Copilot' shown in standalone Copilot
recommended_setting: On — reduces AI surfacing of ungoverned items
notes: Without this, Copilot may surface ungoverned/uncertified items to users
ms_docs_anchor: Only show approved items in the standalone Copilot in Power BI experience (preview)

name: Users can use Azure Maps services
description: Users can access features powered by Azure Maps services
notes: Azure Maps in Fabric; Microsoft-managed
ms_docs_anchor: Users can use Azure Maps services

name: Azure Maps data processed outside capacity geography
description: Data sent to Azure Maps can be processed outside capacity region
notes: Same as integration version; data residency risk for EU tenants
ms_docs_anchor: Data sent to Azure Maps can be processed outside your capacity's geographic region, compliance boundary or national cloud instance

name: Users can use Azure Maps Weather Services (Preview)
description: Users can access weather data from Azure Maps Weather (AccuWeather)
notes: AccuWeather data via Azure Maps; low risk
ms_docs_anchor: Users can use Azure Maps Weather Services (Preview)

name: Workspace admins can add/remove additional workloads (preview)
description: Workspace admins can add/remove workloads in their workspaces
recommended_setting: Off — workload partners not validated by Microsoft
notes: Third-party workloads receive user tokens; vet publishers carefully
ms_docs_anchor: Workspace admins can add and remove additional workloads (preview)

name: Capacity admins can add/remove additional workloads
description: Capacity admins or contributors can add/remove workloads in capacities
recommended_setting: Off unless specific certified workloads needed
notes: Same concern as workspace-level; capacity scope is broader
ms_docs_anchor: Capacity admins and contributors can add and remove additional workloads

name: Workspace admins can develop partner workloads
description: Workspace admins can develop partner workloads with local dev environment
recommended_setting: Off for production; On for dev/sandbox only
notes: Development scenario; not for production tenants
ms_docs_anchor: Workspace admins can develop partner workloads

name: Users can see workloads not validated by Microsoft
description: Users can see and work with additional workloads not validated by Microsoft
recommended_setting: Off — keep disabled until partner is vetted
notes: Unvalidated third-party workloads = uncontrolled data access by external publishers
ms_docs_anchor: Users can see and work with additional workloads not validated by Microsoft

name: Allow non-Entra ID auth in Eventstream
notes: New setting detected — pending human review.
ms_docs_anchor: Allow non-Entra ID auth in Eventstream

name: ArcGIS GeoAnalytics for Fabric Runtime
notes: New setting detected — pending human review.
ms_docs_anchor: ArcGIS GeoAnalytics for Fabric Runtime

name: Data sent to Azure Maps can be processed by Microsoft Online Services Subprocessors
notes: New setting detected — pending human review.
ms_docs_anchor: Data sent to Azure Maps can be processed by Microsoft Online Services Subprocessors

name: Define maximum number of Fabric identities in a tenant
notes: New setting detected — pending human review.
ms_docs_anchor: Define maximum number of Fabric identities in a tenant

name: Dremio SSO
notes: New setting detected — pending human review.
ms_docs_anchor: Dremio SSO

name: Redshift SSO
notes: New setting detected — pending human review.
ms_docs_anchor: Redshift SSO

name: Users can share links to Power BI files stored in OneDrive and SharePoint through Power BI Desktop (preview)
notes: New setting detected — pending human review.
ms_docs_anchor: Users can share links to Power BI files stored in OneDrive and SharePoint through Power BI Desktop (preview)
